digest.component_or_area: config discovery / tombstone registry for removed env and TOML inputs
propositions: Removed env vars are fully silent (not in alias table, fall through to defaults) and removed TOML keys only get a generic unknown-key warning that never names the replacement, silently redirecting the store or weakening security/policy surfaces.

A RemovedInput tombstone registry (kind env|cfg|file, name, replacement, removed_in, behavior error|warn) must fire at config discovery and CLI startup before defaults are selected, turning retired inputs into a targeted migration error naming old name, replacement, and removed-in version.

The tombstone raises RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException (not Exception or ConfigError) so it cannot be swallowed by tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError fallback or the MCP boot's broad except Exception handlers.

The env-var tombstone check must be placed at the top of load_config() before the _RESULT_CACHE lookup and added to _env_signature() so it fires even on a warm cache hit in the long-running MCP server.

The MCP-server write path must fail startup/the request hard on a retired error-class input rather than downgrade to a per-request error, with mcp_server.py handlers re-raising RemovedInputError explicitly.

A CI anti-regression check compares recognised config keys/env vars against a committed golden list and fails if a key is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry, while genuinely-unknown keys keep the existing forward-compat policy.
